zh.lifeSummary: 當John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin於1352年出生在英國史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔時，他的父親Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin25歲，母親Aleyne FitzAlan29歲。他於1369年6月13日與Maud de Mohun在英國史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1397年7月28日在他的家鄉享年45歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin于1352年出生在英国史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔时，他的父亲Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin25岁，母亲Aleyne FitzAlan29岁。他于1369年6月13日与Maud de Mohun在英国史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1397年7月28日在他的家乡享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin was born in 1352, in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, his father, Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin, was 25 and his mother, Aleyne FitzAlan, was 29. He married Maud de Mohun on 13 June 1369, in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 28 July 1397, in his hometown, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin est né en 1352, à Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Aleyne FitzAlan, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Mohun le 13 juin 1369, à Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 28 juillet 1397, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin 1352 in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin 25 und seine Mutter Aleyne FitzAlan 29. Er hat am 13. Juni 1369 in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England Maud de Mohun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 28. Juli 1397 im Alter von 45 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin è nato il nel 1352 nel Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Aleyne FitzAlan, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Maud de Mohun il 13 giugno 1369, nel Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 28 luglio 1397, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 45 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockinが1352年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアノクイン イン オスウエストリーで生まれた時，父Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockinは25歳，母Aleyne FitzAlanは29歳であった。1369年6月13日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアノクイン イン オスウエストリーでMaud de Mohunと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1397年7月28日に故郷で45歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin(이)가 1352년 영국 슈럽실르 노킨 인 오스베스트리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Aleyne FitzAlan(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1369년 6월 13일 영국 슈럽실르 노킨 인 오스베스트리에서 Maud de Mohun(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1397년 7월 28일 그의 고향에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin nasceu em 1352, em Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Aleyne FitzAlan, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud de Mohun em 13 de junho de 1369, em Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de julho de 1397, em sua cidade natal, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin родился в 1352 г. в местности ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Aleyne FitzAlan, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Mohun 13 июня 1369 г. в местности ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 28 июля 1397 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John le Strange 6th Lord Strange of Knockin nació en 1352, en Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Roger le Strange 5th Lord Strange of Knockin, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Aleyne FitzAlan, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Maud de Mohun el 13 de junio de 1369, en Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 28 de julio de 1397, en su pueblo, a la edad de 45 años.
